page: Amazon Web Services m6a.12xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor. This x86_64 architecture system features 48 virtual CPUs across 24 dedicated physical cores, operating at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz with hyperthreading enabled. The memory configuration consists of 192.0 GB of DDR4 RAM running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached resources, and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the m6a.12xlarge demonstrates strong CPU performance, placing in the top 25% for both single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests, as well as the Passmark CPU score. It also shows strong capabilities in cryptographic operations, ray tracing, image processing, and text generation for small language models. Memory performance is robust, with a Passmark memory score in the top 25%, though its raw memory bandwidth and database operations fall within the average range. Other workloads, such as software compilation, static web serving, and medium-to-large language model inference, consistently register average performance.

The absence of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPUs represents a clear tradeoff, making this instance less suitable for high-IOPS database workloads or heavy parallel computing tasks. However, its balanced resource allocation and strong CPU capabilities offer solid qualitative cost efficiency for general-purpose workloads. The instance is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, web serving, software compilation, and moderate-scale enterprise applications that require a stable balance of compute and memory without the need for local storage.
description: Amazon Web Services m6a.12xlarge is a general-purpose server utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 48 vCPUs (24 physical cores) running at 3.6 GHz, paired with 192.0 GB of DDR4 RAM. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, but provides a 19 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks indicate strong CPU performance, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng and Passmark CPU tests, alongside strong cryptographic and image processing capabilities. Memory bandwidth and database operations perform in the average tier. This balanced resource profile makes the m6a.12xlarge qualitatively cost-efficient for general-purpose enterprise applications, multi-threaded processing, software compilation, and web serving workloads that do not require local NVMe storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6a.12xlarge is a general-purpose server with 48 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7R13 CPU, delivering strong compute performance for balanced enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6a.12xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 48 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 3.6 GHz AMD EPYC processor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and DDR4 memory for reliable multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores
Equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 RAM and 19 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Delivers strong top-tier CPU performance in stress-ng and Passmark benchmarks
Lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware
Best suited for general-purpose enterprise applications, web serving, and software compilation
